title: AI Worker Task Contract
description: Control-plane schema for self-hosted AI batch launcher task definitions. This schema covers scheduling metadata (file boundaries, budgets, conflict groups, risk, validation commands, role routing) — not task semantics. Workers must read the GitHub issue body and sourceOfTruthDocs for task understanding. Aligned with docs/ai-native/worker-task-contract.md.

taskType.description: execution = write code/docs, research = read-only, review = evaluate existing work

risk.description: low = docs/config, medium = cross-module, high = auth/data/public API

conflictGroup.description: Tasks in the same group must not run concurrently

targetPR.description: Target PR number if updating an existing PR, null for new work

expectedPR.description: Whether this task should produce a PR

allowedFiles.description: Glob patterns the worker MAY edit

forbiddenFiles.description: Glob patterns the worker MUST NOT edit, even if inside allowedFiles

validationCommands.description: Shell commands the worker must run before opening a PR

description.description: One-line summary of what this worker does

knownBlindspots.description: Patterns or mistakes to actively avoid

sourceIssue.description: GitHub issue URL — the semantic source of truth for this task. Workers MUST read the issue body before starting work.

knowledgeRefs.description: File paths or URLs the worker should read for semantic context (issue body, contracts, architecture docs). Not a control field — pointers to sources the worker reads at runtime.

promptHandoff.description: Concise description of what to build and why. Derived from the issue title or body. Workers use this as the initial prompt context; full details live in sourceIssue and knowledgeRefs.

llmExtracted.description: True when the task JSON was produced by LLM extraction (e.g. Claude parsed the issue body). Absent or false means the fields came from deterministic/structured input. Workers use this to decide whether richer semantic fields (knowledgeRefs, promptHandoff, attentionAreas) are trustworthy.
